curves.description: 32-bit-ish prime-sized coefficients without an obvious rational root

curves.description: large 32-bit a with small constant for fast negative rational-root sanity check
curves.source_note: a=2^31-1, b=1; rational root candidates +/-1 fail

curves.description: mixed-sign 32-bit-ish stress curve with one rational 2-torsion point
curves.source_note: a=-2^31, b=2^31-1; deterministic boundary-value coefficients

curves.description: controlled full rational 2-torsion with large roots
curves.source_note: constructed as (x+257)(x-2)(x-255), roots sum to zero

curves.description: controlled one rational 2-torsion with large coefficients
curves.source_note: constructed as (x-r)(x^2+r*x+c) with r=65537, c=104729

curves.description: 64-bit-ish prime-offset coefficients without an obvious rational root

curves.source_note: a is near 2^63; rational root candidates +/-1 fail

curves.description: mixed 64-bit-ish coefficients

curves.description: controlled full rational 2-torsion with large roots
curves.source_note: constructed as (x+65537)(x-2)(x-65535), roots sum to zero

curves.description: scaled order-four probe example from y^2=x^3+4x
curves.source_note: integer scaling x=u^2*x0,y=u^3*y0 from y^2=x^3+4x with u=65537

curves.description: 128-bit-ish a with small b for fast negative rational-root check
curves.source_note: a=2^127-1, b=1; rational root candidates +/-1 fail

curves.description: large 128-bit-ish coefficient pair
curves.source_note: deterministic powers-of-two offsets; intended as rational diagnostic stress example

curves.description: negative/positive 128-bit-ish mixed coefficients
curves.source_note: deterministic boundary-value coefficients over Q

curves.description: controlled full rational 2-torsion with large roots
curves.source_note: constructed as (x+(2^32+15))(x-2)(x-(2^32+13)), roots sum to zero

curves.description: scaled order-four probe example from y^2=x^3+4x
curves.source_note: integer scaling x=u^2*x0,y=u^3*y0 from y^2=x^3+4x with u=2^32+15

curves.source_note: moderate integer scaling of y^2=x^3+4x for bounded order-four probe
